fl-q2.text: How much oil is on the ground?

fl-t1.text: Coolant leak on LMTV requires maintenance support.

fl-t2.text: Transmission fluid leak. Do not operate.

fl-t3.text: Brake fluid leak detected. Safety-critical — do not operate.

fl-t4.text: Minor oil seepage (dampness, no drips) is within normal limits. Note and monitor.

terminal.summary: Minor oil seepage — within limits, monitoring

fl-t5.text: Steady oil drip or puddle found. Needs maintenance inspection.

terminal.summary: Steady oil drip or puddle — needs maintenance

td-t2.text: Inflate tire using CTIS or external air. Monitor for leaks.

ws-q1.text: Does the engine crank when you turn the key to START?

options.label: No — engine does not crank at all
Yes — engine cranks but does not start

ws-q2.text: Check battery cable connections. Are they tight and free of corrosion?

ws-a1.actionText: Disconnect cables (negative first). Clean terminals and cable clamps with a wire brush or battery cleaning tool. Reconnect cables (positive first), tighten securely. Attempt to start.

ws-q3.text: Check battery fluid level. Are cells filled to the proper level?

ws-a2.actionText: Add distilled water to each cell until the fluid just covers the plates. Do not overfill. Allow 15 minutes, then attempt to start.

ws-t1.text: Battery cables were corroded/loose. Cleaning and tightening resolved the issue. If engine cranks but does not start, continue troubleshooting fuel system.

ws-t2.text: Engine does not crank despite good battery cables and fluid. Notify unit maintenance.

ws-t3.text: Battery fluid was low. Topped off and engine now cranks.

terminal.summary: Battery fluid topped off — engine cranks

ws-q5.text: Check the fuel level. Is there fuel in the tank?

options.label: Tank is empty or very low

ws-a3.actionText: Fill the fuel tank with JP-8 or diesel. After fueling, prime the fuel/water separator by pumping the primer handle until firm. Attempt to start.

ws-t4.text: Fuel tank was empty. Refueled and engine starts normally.

terminal.summary: Fuel tank was empty — refueled, engine starts

ws-q6.text: Check fuel/water separator. Does the bowl show water contamination or need priming?

options.label: Water visible in bowl or needs priming

ws-a4.text: Drain and prime the fuel/water separator
ws-a4.actionText: Open the drain valve on the fuel/water separator and drain any water. Close the valve. Pump the manual primer handle until firm resistance is felt. Attempt to start.

ws-t5.text: Fuel/water separator was contaminated. Drained and primed — engine starts.

terminal.summary: Fuel/water separator drained and primed — engine starts

ws-q8.text: Check the glow plug relay/wait-to-start indicator. Did the WAIT TO START light illuminate before cranking?

options.label: No — light did not come on

ws-t6.text: Engine cranks but will not start despite fuel and electrical checks. Notify unit maintenance.

terminal.summary: Engine cranks, won't start — needs maintenance

options.label: Nothing works — no lights, no gauges, no start
Some things work but others don't
A gauge or indicator isn't working

el-q2.text: Check battery cable connections at batteries and junction block. Are they tight and corrosion-free?

el-a1.actionText: Disconnect cables (negative first). Clean terminals and clamps with wire brush. Reconnect (positive first) and tighten. Check if circuits restore.

el-q6.text: Are the electrical circuits working now?

el-t1.text: Battery cables were corroded or loose. Cleaning and tightening restored power.

el-q3.text: Check fuses in the main fuse panel. Are any blown?

el-t2.text: Blown fuse found. Notify unit maintenance for replacement and root cause diagnosis.

el-t3.text: Everything still dead despite good cables and fuses. Notify unit maintenance.

el-q4.text: Check battery cables at the cable junction under the hood. Are connections tight?

el-a2.actionText: Tighten the cable connections at the cable junction under the hood. Check if the affected systems restore.

el-q7.text: Are the affected systems working now?

el-t5.text: Loose cable connection found and tightened. Affected systems restored.

el-t4.text: Affected systems still not operating despite good connections. Notify unit maintenance.

el-q5.text: Which gauge or indicator has failed?

options.label: Warning light stays on or won't illuminate

el-t6.text: Individual gauge or indicator failure. Notify unit maintenance.

el-t7.text: Engine temperature or oil pressure gauge not working. Safety-critical — do not operate without these instruments. Notify unit maintenance.

terminal.summary: Temp/oil pressure gauge failure — NMC, safety-critical

un-q1.text: What type of unusual noise or vibration are you experiencing?

options.label: Engine misfires, runs rough, or lacks power

un-q2.text: Check the air filter. Is it dirty or restricted?

un-a1.text: Clean or replace the air filter
un-a1.actionText: Remove the air filter element. If a dry-type filter, tap gently to remove loose dirt. Reinstall or replace with a clean filter from BII. Check if vibration reduces.

un-q3a.text: Is the vibration reduced after cleaning the air filter?

un-t3.text: Dirty air filter was causing vibration. Cleaned/replaced filter.

un-q3.text: Visually check the vibration damper mounting bolts. Are any loose or missing?

un-t4.text: Excessive vibration persists. Notify unit maintenance for inspection of vibration damper and engine mounts.

un-q4.text: Check the air filter. Is it dirty or restricted?

un-a2.text: Clean or replace the air filter
un-a2.actionText: Remove the air filter element. Tap gently to remove loose dirt or replace with a clean filter. Reinstall and check engine performance.

un-q5a.text: Is the engine running smoother now?

un-t5.text: Dirty air filter was causing rough running. Cleaned/replaced filter.

terminal.summary: Air filter cleaned — engine runs smoother

un-q5.text: Check for visible fuel leaks around fuel lines and connections.

un-t6.text: Fuel leak found. Do not operate. Notify unit maintenance immediately.

terminal.summary: Fuel leak causing rough running — NMC

un-t7.text: Engine misfires or runs rough with no operator-correctable cause found. Notify unit maintenance.

un-q6.text: Check the air filter. Is it dirty or restricted?

un-a3.text: Clean or replace the air filter
un-a3.actionText: Remove and clean or replace the air filter element. Reinstall and check if engine holds idle.

un-q7a.text: Does the engine hold idle without stalling?

un-t8.text: Dirty air filter was causing low RPM stall. Cleaned/replaced filter.

terminal.summary: Air filter cleaned — engine holds idle

un-q7.text: Check intake and turbo hoses for kinks, cracks, or disconnections.

un-t9.text: Engine stalls at low RPM. Notify unit maintenance.

terminal.summary: Engine stalls at low RPM — needs maintenance

un-t1.text: Unusually noisy exhaust indicates exhaust system damage. Notify unit maintenance.

un-t2.text: Exhaust fumes in cab are a safety hazard. Stop vehicle immediately. Ventilate cab. Do not operate until repaired. Notify unit maintenance.

terminal.summary: Exhaust fumes in cab — NMC, safety hazard

oh-q1.text: Is the engine currently overheating (temp gauge in red) or are you troubleshooting coolant loss?

oh-a1.actionText: Stop the vehicle in a safe location. Let the engine idle for 2-3 minutes, then shut off. Allow to cool for at least 15 minutes before opening the cooling system. WARNING: Do not remove the radiator cap while engine is hot — risk of severe burns.

oh-q2.text: Once cooled, check the coolant level in the overflow tank. Is it low or empty?

oh-a2.actionText: Add coolant to the overflow tank up to the FULL mark. If no coolant is available, use clean water as an emergency measure. Check for visible leaks around radiator, hoses, and connections.

oh-q3.text: Check the radiator cap. Is it properly seated and does the seal look intact?

options.label: Cap is loose or seal looks damaged

oh-a3.actionText: Press down on the radiator cap and turn clockwise until it locks. If the cap seal is visibly damaged, replace with a spare if available.

oh-q4.text: Check the radiator fins. Are they clogged with debris (mud, bugs, leaves)?

oh-a4.actionText: Remove debris from the front of the radiator using a soft brush or compressed air (blow from engine side outward). Be careful not to bend the fins.

oh-q5.text: Check all hose connections (upper and lower radiator hoses, heater hoses). Are any loose or leaking?

oh-a5.actionText: Tighten the loose hose clamp(s) with a screwdriver. Wipe the area dry and watch for continued leaking.

oh-q6.text: After performing all checks above, does the cooling system appear serviceable?

options.label: Yes — topped off coolant, cleared fins, tightened hoses
No — still leaking or cannot determine cause

oh-t1.text: Cooling system topped off and checked. Monitor temperature gauge closely during operation. If overheating recurs, stop and notify maintenance.

terminal.summary: Coolant topped off, cooling system checked — monitor temp

oh-t2.text: Cooling system has an unresolvable issue at operator level. Do not continue operating. Notify unit maintenance.

op-q1.text: What oil-related symptom are you experiencing?

op-q2.text: Stop the engine. Check oil level on the dipstick. What is the level?

op-a1.actionText: Add the correct weight engine oil (15W-40) until the dipstick reads between ADD and FULL. Do not overfill. Start the engine and check if oil pressure returns to normal.

op-q2a.text: Did oil pressure return to normal after adding oil?

op-t1.text: Oil level was low. Topped off and pressure is normal. Monitor for consumption and leaks.

terminal.summary: Oil level topped off — pressure normal, monitor

op-q3.text: Check the oil on the dipstick. Does it look milky, foamy, or smell like fuel?

op-t2.text: Engine oil is contaminated (possible coolant or fuel intrusion). Do not operate. Notify unit maintenance immediately.

terminal.summary: Contaminated engine oil — NMC, needs maintenance

op-t3.text: Low oil pressure with adequate, uncontaminated oil. Possible sensor or pump issue. Do not operate. Notify unit maintenance.

op-q4.text: Check oil level on dipstick. Then check around the engine for visible oil leaks — steady drip or puddle under the vehicle.

options.label: No visible leaks — oil just low

op-a2.text: Top off engine oil and monitor
op-a2.actionText: Add oil to bring the level between ADD and FULL on the dipstick. Record the mileage and check again in 100 miles to determine consumption rate.

op-t4.text: Oil leak causing excessive consumption. Notify unit maintenance.

terminal.summary: Oil leak — excessive consumption, needs maintenance

op-t5.text: Oil topped off. No visible leak found but consumption is high. Notify unit maintenance for further diagnosis.

terminal.summary: Excessive oil consumption — topped off, needs maintenance

op-q5.text: Blue exhaust smoke indicates oil burning. Check oil level on the dipstick.

op-a3.actionText: Add engine oil to bring the level between ADD and FULL on the dipstick.

op-t6.text: Blue exhaust smoke indicates internal oil burning. Notify unit maintenance.

terminal.summary: Blue exhaust smoke — oil burning, needs maintenance

es-q1.text: What color is the exhaust smoke, or what is the engine symptom?

es-q2.text: Black/gray smoke usually means incomplete combustion. Check the air filter — is it dirty or restricted?

es-a1.text: Clean or replace the air filter
es-a1.actionText: Remove the air filter element. Clean or replace it. Reinstall and check if smoke clears during operation.

es-q3a.text: Did the smoke clear after cleaning the air filter?

es-t2.text: Dirty air filter was causing excessive smoke. Cleaned/replaced filter.

es-q3.text: Check intake and turbo hoses for kinks, cracks, or disconnections.

es-t3.text: Excessive black/gray exhaust smoke persists. Notify unit maintenance.

es-q4.text: White smoke can indicate cold start or coolant leak. Check the air filter — is it dirty?

es-a2.text: Clean or replace the air filter
es-a2.actionText: Remove, clean or replace the air filter element.

es-q4a.text: Did white smoke clear after cleaning the air filter?

es-t5.text: Air filter was causing white smoke. Cleaned/replaced.

terminal.summary: Air filter cleaned — white smoke cleared

es-t4.text: White exhaust smoke persists. Possible coolant leak into combustion chamber. Notify unit maintenance.

es-t1.text: Engine overspeeds on start — this is a dangerous condition. Shut off immediately. Do not attempt to restart. Notify unit maintenance.

terminal.summary: Engine overspeeds — NMC, dangerous, needs maintenance

es-q5.text: Low engine power. Check for fuel contamination — is there water or sediment in the fuel/water separator?

es-a3.actionText: Open the drain valve on the fuel/water separator and drain contaminated fuel into a container. Close valve. Prime the separator by pumping the handle until firm.

es-q6.text: Check oil level on the dipstick.

es-a4.actionText: Add correct weight engine oil until dipstick reads between ADD and FULL.

es-q7.text: Check the air filter. Is it dirty or restricted?

es-a5.actionText: Remove and clean or replace the air filter element.

es-q8.text: Check intake and turbo hoses for kinks or disconnections.

es-t6.text: Low engine power persists after operator checks. Notify unit maintenance.

bi-q1.text: What brake symptom are you experiencing?

options.label: Brakes do not hold / excessive pedal travel
Brakes grab or pull to one side

bi-q2.text: Check the brake fluid reservoir. Is the fluid level low?

bi-t1.text: Brake system malfunction. This is safety-critical — do not operate. Notify unit maintenance immediately.

bi-t2.text: Unusual brake noise indicates possible worn pads or hardware issue. Notify unit maintenance.

terminal.summary: Unusual brake noise — needs maintenance inspection

bi-t3.text: Low brake fluid with warning light. Possible leak in the brake system. Do not operate. Notify unit maintenance.

terminal.summary: Low brake fluid — NMC, possible leak, needs maintenance

tr-q1.text: What transmission or drivetrain symptom are you experiencing?

options.label: Transmission will not shift / hard shifting
Unusual noise from transmission or transfer case

tr-t1.text: Transmission malfunction is beyond operator-level repair. Notify unit maintenance.

tr-t2.text: Transfer case engagement issue. Stop and notify unit maintenance.

tr-t3.text: Transmission fluid leak detected. Do not operate. Notify unit maintenance.

tr-t4.text: Transmission overheating. Stop vehicle and allow to cool. Notify unit maintenance.

st-q1.text: What steering symptom are you experiencing?

options.label: Hard to steer / power steering not working

st-q2.text: Check the power steering fluid reservoir. Is the fluid level low?

st-t1.text: Steering system malfunction. This is safety-critical. Notify unit maintenance.

st-t2.text: Power steering fluid low or leaking. Do not operate without power steering — steering effort is unsafe. Notify unit maintenance.

terminal.summary: Power steering fluid low/leak — NMC, needs maintenance

fs-q1.text: What fuel system issue are you experiencing?

options.label: Ether starting aid does not work

fs-q2.text: Check around fuel tank, lines, and connections for visible fuel leaks.

fs-t1.text: Ether starting aid inoperative. Notify unit maintenance.

fs-t2.text: Sticking accelerator pedal is a safety hazard. Do not operate. Notify unit maintenance immediately.

fs-t3.text: Fuel leak found. Fire hazard — do not operate. Notify unit maintenance immediately.

fs-t4.text: High fuel consumption with no visible leaks. Notify unit maintenance for diagnosis.

cs-q1.text: Is there oil visible in the cooling system (milky residue in overflow tank or radiator)?

cs-t1.text: Oil in cooling system indicates internal engine or oil cooler leak. Do not operate. Notify unit maintenance immediately.

terminal.summary: Oil in cooling system — NMC, needs maintenance

cs-t2.text: Coolant appears normal. If you are experiencing overheating, use the Engine Overheat troubleshooting tree.

terminal.summary: Cooling system appears normal — see engine-overheat if needed
